name: playbooks
context: Author, validate, preview, and execute reusable Ansible playbooks against controlled host subsets
info: Playbooks are the durable execution layer in Ansible: YAML documents that target inventory groups, call modules in order, trigger handlers, consume vars files, and expose safety flags such as syntax check, list-tasks, list-tags, check mode, and diff mode. This skill is for the jobs agents perform repeatedly in production: run a version-controlled playbook, preview its impact, scope it to a canary or tag, feed it Vault secrets safely, and understand the execution controls that keep rollouts predictable. When ad-hoc work becomes repeatable, reviewed, or multi-step, it belongs here.
triggers: limit a playbook to canary hosts
execute a playbook with vault secrets
body:
# Ansible Playbooks

Playbooks are how Ansible becomes a maintainable engineering system instead of a pile of one-liners. They define target hosts, privilege model, variables, and ordered tasks in YAML, then run them with `ansible-playbook`.

## Minimal Example

```yaml
- name: Configure web servers
  hosts: web
  become: true
  tasks:
    - name: Ensure nginx is present
      ansible.builtin.package:
        name: nginx
        state: present

    - name: Deploy nginx config
      ansible.builtin.template:
        src: templates/nginx.conf.j2
        dest: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
        mode: '0644'
      notify: restart nginx

  handlers:
    - name: restart nginx
      ansible.builtin.service:
        name: nginx
        state: restarted
```

## Execution Controls

```bash
ansible-playbook -i inventory site.yml
ansible-playbook -i inventory site.yml --syntax-check
ansible-playbook -i inventory site.yml --list-tasks --list-tags
ansible-playbook -i inventory site.yml -C -D
ansible-playbook -i inventory site.yml -l canary -t packages
ansible-playbook -i inventory site.yml --start-at-task 'Deploy nginx config'
ansible-playbook -i inventory site.yml --vault-id prod@prompt
```

## Project Layout

```text
ansible-project/
  inventory/
  group_vars/
  host_vars/
  roles/
  templates/
  files/
  site.yml
```

## When To Promote From Ad-Hoc

If a task will be repeated, reviewed in Git, needs handlers, or must run in a specific order, convert it from [[ansible/ad-hoc]] into a playbook. That is the boundary between convenient execution and operational discipline. Pair it with [[ansible/inventory]] for targeting, [[ansible/vault]] for secrets, and [[ansible/collections]] for module availability.

sections.title: Plays, tasks, and handlers
sections.body: A playbook is a list of plays. Each play targets `hosts`, can set `become`, variables, and fact gathering behavior, and contains ordered `tasks`. Handlers run only when notified, which makes them the right place for restarts and reloads. This structure gives you idempotent config changes without unnecessary service churn.

sections.title: syntax-check, list-tasks, check, and diff
sections.body: Use `--syntax-check` before the first run, `--list-tasks` and `--list-tags` to inspect scope, `-C` or `--check` to preview supported changes, and `-D` or `--diff` to inspect file or template deltas. These flags are the main safety controls for agent-driven runs. Pair them with limited host subsets for high-risk changes.

sections.title: Use tags and limits for staged rollout
sections.body: Tags let you run only part of a playbook with `-t` or skip parts with `--skip-tags`. Limits such as `-l canary` constrain the host set after inventory resolution. Together they support progressive rollout: validate the playbook on one host or one task family, then widen the scope after the result looks correct.

sections.title: Playbooks and Vault secrets
sections.body: Playbooks commonly load secret data from `group_vars`, `host_vars`, or `vars_files` encrypted with [[ansible/vault]]. Supply secrets with `--vault-id` or `--vault-password-file`, not by hardcoding plaintext values into YAML. Use `no_log: true` on tasks that may render secret values in stdout.

scripts.name: Run Playbook
scripts.description: Execute ansible-playbook with syntax checks, limits, tags, check mode, and vault options

ANSIBLE_PLAYBOOK_FILE.description: Path to the playbook file to execute

ANSIBLE_INVENTORY_SOURCE.description: Inventory file, directory, or plugin source

ANSIBLE_PLAYBOOK_CHECK.description: Set to true to run ansible-playbook in check mode

ANSIBLE_SYNTAX_CHECK.description: Set to true to validate playbook syntax only

ANSIBLE_PLAYBOOK_DIFF.description: Set to true to show template and file diffs

ANSIBLE_START_AT_TASK.description: Resume execution at the named task

ANSIBLE_EXTRA_VARS.description: Extra vars passed with -e in key=value or JSON form

ANSIBLE_VAULT_PASSWORD_FILE.description: Path to a vault password file

ANSIBLE_LIMIT.description: Host limit applied after inventory resolution

inventorySource.description: Inventory file, directory, or plugin source

diff.description: Show diffs for changed small files and templates

command.description: The ansible-playbook command that was executed

depends.reason: Playbook host selection and variables come from inventory
Encrypted vars files often need vault identities during playbook execution
